2024/2025 Drought-net

Janelle Bohey

2026-01-27

Packages

library(bipartite)

#rainout treatments

#treatments 
#loads in plot and transect codes paired with treatments (rainout, control)
rainout.treatments <- read_sheet("1YiOm8el0IGCvUHm8xDRxf8U9IWiwI8uNT6KGCzZP2sY") %>% 
                  mutate(transectplot = as.character(transectplot))
## ! Using an auto-discovered, cached token.

#Flower Counts

Data Cleaning

focalspecies.2024 <- c("delphinium", "erigeron_flagellaris", "umbellatum", "eremogone_congesta", "potentilla_hippiana", "galium_boreale", "heterotheca_villosa", "heliomeris_multiflora")
focalspecies.2025 <- c("clay","delphinium", "umb", "flag", "pot", "congesta", "het", "helio", "galium" )

flowercounts.2024 <- read_sheet("1YR7lBNf5s9Drw9Ix61J8wUtKQO-XuZmXrRMhgktsc5k", sheet="2024") %>%
                      filter(plant!="na") %>%  #2024-07-02, 4f - no flowers open wrote na
                      mutate(transectplot=paste0(transect,plot)) %>%
                      left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
                      mutate(week_num = factor(week(date)-26)) %>% 
                      group_by(date, week_num, transect, plot,transectplot, plant, treatment) %>% 
                      filter(! (week_num== "1"& scribe =="sb")) %>% #removes sb's duplicate flower counts in week 1
                      summarize(flower_number=mean(flower_number)) %>% 
                      filter(plant%in% focalspecies.2024) %>% 
                      #rename plant names
                      mutate(plant = dplyr::recode(plant,
                                           "erigeron_flagellaris" ="flag" ,
                                           "eremogone_congesta" = "congesta",
                                           "potentilla_hippiana" = "potentilla",
                                           "galium_boreale" = "galium",
                                           "heterotheca_villosa" = "heterotheca",
                                           "heliomeris_multiflora" = "heliomeris")) %>% 
                      mutate(year = 2024) %>%   
                      ungroup()
## ✔ Reading from "Flower Number Data".
## ✔ Range ''2024''.
## Joining with `by = join_by(transect, plot, transectplot)`
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'date', 'week_num', 'transect', 'plot',
## 'transectplot', 'plant'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
flowercounts.2025 <- read_sheet("1ObUirLHnFpHzpepx_tiXLPFxXfSVSJ6s9IAa4_Kd25o", na="") %>% 
                      mutate(flower_number = as.numeric(flower_number)) %>%
                      left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
                      group_by(date, week_num_flowcounts, transect, plot,transectplot, plant, treatment) %>% 
                      summarize(flower_number=mean(flower_number)) %>% 
                      #remove species with little or no data:
                      filter(plant%in% focalspecies.2025) %>% 
                      filter(!plant%in% "clay") %>% #remove claytonia becuase not measured in 2024
                      
                      mutate(plant = dplyr::recode(plant,
                                            "clay" = "claytonia",
                                            "umb" = "umbellatum",
                                            "pot" = "potentilla",
                                            "het" = "heterotheca",
                                            "helio" = "heliomeris")) %>% 
                        mutate(year = 2025) %>% 
                        ungroup()
## ✔ Reading from "vrate_flower_number_2025".
## ✔ Range 'Sheet1'.
## Joining with `by = join_by(transect, plot, transectplot)``summarise()` has grouped output by 'date', 'week_num_flowcounts', 'transect', 'plot', 'transectplot', 'plant'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
setdiff( flowercounts.2025$plant, flowercounts.2024$plant)
## character(0)
library(lubridate)

all.flowercounts <- bind_rows(flowercounts.2024, flowercounts.2025) %>% 
                            select(-week_num, -week_num_flowcounts) %>% 
                            mutate(calendar_week = week(date))  #adds calendar week based on the date column
                          
  
#TODO:Does flower number differ by year (per species); Is flower timining different (onset of timing) between years.
unique(all.flowercounts$plant)
## [1] "delphinium"  "flag"        "umbellatum"  "congesta"    "potentilla" 
## [6] "galium"      "heterotheca" "heliomeris"

##Does flower number differ by year (per species)

avg.flowers.year <- all.flowercounts %>% group_by(plant, year) %>%
                            summarize(mean_flowers = mean(flower_number, na.rm = TRUE),
                                sd_flowers = sd(flower_number, na.rm = TRUE),
                                n = n())
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'plant'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
ggplot(all.flowercounts, aes(x = factor(year), y = flower_number, fill = factor(year))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~plant, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Flower number", fill = "Year")
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

all.flower.year.lm <- lm(flower_number ~ plant * factor(year), data = all.flowercounts)
summary(all.flower.year.lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = flower_number ~ plant * factor(year), data = all.flowercounts)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
## -78.47 -22.01  -8.51   9.41 516.75 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                         25.752      4.630   5.562 3.00e-08 ***
## plantdelphinium                    -23.324     19.171  -1.217 0.223893    
## plantflag                           -8.120      6.655  -1.220 0.222573    
## plantgalium                         -1.502      7.246  -0.207 0.835776    
## plantheliomeris                     12.460      7.192   1.733 0.083320 .  
## plantheterotheca                    53.719      5.684   9.451  < 2e-16 ***
## plantpotentilla                    -16.544      6.832  -2.422 0.015537 *  
## plantumbellatum                    -11.161      6.520  -1.712 0.087064 .  
## factor(year)2025                    27.970      6.255   4.472 8.16e-06 ***
## plantdelphinium:factor(year)2025    38.037     19.889   1.912 0.055951 .  
## plantflag:factor(year)2025         -21.597      8.673  -2.490 0.012842 *  
## plantgalium:factor(year)2025       -40.190     10.339  -3.887 0.000104 ***
## plantheliomeris:factor(year)2025   -46.735      9.502  -4.918 9.39e-07 ***
## plantheterotheca:factor(year)2025  -50.738      7.581  -6.693 2.78e-11 ***
## plantpotentilla:factor(year)2025   -26.621      9.051  -2.941 0.003303 ** 
## plantumbellatum:factor(year)2025   -26.259      8.669  -3.029 0.002481 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 49.22 on 2162 degrees of freedom
##   (1 observation deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1958, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1902 
## F-statistic: 35.08 on 15 and 2162 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
anova(all.flower.year.lm)
## Analysis of Variance Table
## 
## Response: flower_number
##                      Df  Sum Sq Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
## plant                 7 1103392  157627 65.0626 < 2.2e-16 ***
## factor(year)          1    4946    4946  2.0415    0.1532    
## plant:factor(year)    7  166615   23802  9.8246 4.147e-12 ***
## Residuals          2162 5237884    2423                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
all.flower.year.model <- all.flowercounts %>% filter(!plant%in% "delphinium") %>%  
  group_by(plant) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model = map(data, ~ lmer(flower_number ~ factor(year) + (1|transect/transectplot), data = .x)),
    anova_table = map(model, ~ tidy(anova(.x)))  # convert ANOVA to tibble
  ) %>%
  select(plant, anova_table) %>%
  unnest(anova_table) %>%
  mutate(sig = if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", ""))  # add * if p < 0.05
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## Warning: There were 7 warnings in `mutate()`.
## The first warning was:
## ℹ In argument: `anova_table = map(model, ~tidy(anova(.x)))`.
## ℹ In group 1: `plant = "congesta"`.
## Caused by warning in `tidy.anova()`:
## ! The following column names in ANOVA output were not recognized or transformed: NumDF, DenDF
## ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 6 remaining warnings.
View(all.flower.year.model)

#but this model is looking across the entire year, do I need to downsample bc 2024 had much less data???
glmm.all.flower.year.model <- all.flowercounts %>%
  filter(!plant %in% "delphinium") %>%
  group_by(plant) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model = map(data, ~ {
      .x <- .x %>% mutate(flower_number = round(flower_number))
      glmer(flower_number ~ factor(year) + (1 | transectplot),
            family = poisson,
            data = .x)
    }),
    anova_table = map(model, ~ broom::tidy(.x, effects = "fixed"))
  ) %>%
  select(plant, anova_table) %>%
  unnest(anova_table) %>%
  mutate(sig = if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", ""))
View(glmm.all.flower.year.model)

# glmmTMB.flower.year.model <- all.flowercounts %>%
#   filter(!plant %in% "delphinium") %>%

#   nest() %>%
#   mutate(

#       # Round flower counts

#       
#       # Fit GLMM with log-normal (Gaussian on log scale)
#       glmmTMB(flower_number ~ factor(year) + (1 | transectplot),
#               family = gaussian(link = "log"),
#               data = .x)
#     }),
#     
#     # Extract fixed effects estimates in tidy format
#     anova_table = map(model, ~ broom::tidy(.x, effects = "fixed"))
#   ) %>%
#   select(plant, anova_table) %>%
#   unnest(anova_table) %>%
#   mutate(sig = if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", ""))

##Is flower timining different (onset of timing) between years.

flower.timing <- all.flowercounts %>% 
          #filter(!plant%in% "delphinium") %>% #filter delph bc didn't do flower counts early enough in 2024
          group_by(plant, year) %>%
          summarize(
            first_week = min(calendar_week, na.rm = TRUE),  # flowering onset
            last_week  = max(calendar_week, na.rm = TRUE),  # flowering end
            duration   = last_week - first_week + 1,        # flowering duration in weeks
            .groups = "drop")


#Pivot wider to compare 2024 vs 2025
flower.timing.wide <- flower.timing %>%
                      pivot_wider(
                      names_from = year,
                      values_from = c(first_week, last_week, duration),
                      names_sep = "_") %>% 
                    mutate(
                    onset_diff    = first_week_2025 - first_week_2024,
                    end_diff      = last_week_2025 - last_week_2024,
                    duration_diff = duration_2025 - duration_2024)
View(flower.timing.wide)
#Positive values → later start/end or longer duration in 2025.
#Negative values → earlier start/end or shorter duration in 2025.



#plot
flower.timing.long <- flower.timing %>%
        pivot_longer(
          cols = c(first_week, last_week, duration),
          names_to = "metric",
          values_to = "week_value")

ggplot(all.flowercounts,
       aes(x = calendar_week,
           y = flower_number,
           color = plant,
           group = plant)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Calendar week",
    y = "Mean flower number",
    color = "Plant species"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_summary()`).

Morohology

Match 2024 & 2025 data sets merge model for each trait comparing year x trait of each plant species data vis

morph.2024 <- read_sheet("1fJuFDOMitdC6bdTm12XWBTZpX4u15uHdkf0y-Ilelw4") %>% 
                    mutate(plot = tolower(as.character(plot))) %>% 
                    mutate(year = 2024) %>%
                    left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
                    filter(!plant%in% c("micro white?!", " micro white", "pakera",
                                        "campanula", "collomia", "sedum", "subalpinium",
                                        "speciosus", "microwhite","micro white", 
                                        "tall microwhite", "boechera stricta")) %>% 
#some plants don't have length & width measurements, so this code uses only length if width=NA, otherwise takes the average.  
                     mutate(
                        average_flower_size = if_else(
                        is.na(width),
                        length,
                        (length + width) / 2)) 
## ✔ Reading from "Rainout_flower_morphology_2024".
## ✔ Range 'Sheet1'.
## Joining with `by = join_by(transect, plot)`
morph.2025 <- read_sheet("1grngEjxQ6-uulNl_5yYdbmuajY2DXIGkWfHPBVV-3sM", na=c("")) %>%  
            #remove rows with no tag number
                filter( tag != "") %>% 
                filter(!plant%in% c("clay","delphinium")) %>%
                mutate(year = 2025) %>%
                left_join(rainout.treatments)  %>% 
                mutate(average_flower_size = (length+width)/2)
## ✔ Reading from "Morphology 2025".
## ✔ Range 'Sheet1'.
## Warning in .Primitive("as.double")(x, ...): NAs introduced by coercion
## Joining with `by = join_by(transect, plot, transectplot)`
unique(morph.2025$plant)
## [1] "flagellaris" "umbellatum"  "potentilla"  "congesta"    "galium"     
## [6] "heterotheca" "heliomeris"
all.years.morph <- bind_rows(morph.2024,morph.2025) %>% 
                    mutate(calendar_week = week(date)) %>% 
                    select(year, date,calendar_week, observer, transect, transectplot,
                           treatment, plant, tag, depth, length, width, 
                           average_flower_size )  
          
#if you want to add in inflo height list above ^

#Depth: 

depth.all.years.model <- all.years.morph %>% 
  filter(!plant%in% "galium") %>% #galium doesn't have enough data
  group_by(plant) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model = map(data, ~ lmer(depth ~ factor(year) + (1|observer) +(1|transect/transectplot), data = .x)),
    anova_table = map(model, ~ tidy(anova(.x)))  # convert ANOVA to tibble
  ) %>%
  select(plant, anova_table) %>%
  unnest(anova_table) %>%
  mutate(sig = if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", ""))  # add * if p < 0.05
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## Warning: There were 6 warnings in `mutate()`.
## The first warning was:
## ℹ In argument: `anova_table = map(model, ~tidy(anova(.x)))`.
## ℹ In group 1: `plant = "congesta"`.
## Caused by warning in `tidy.anova()`:
## ! The following column names in ANOVA output were not recognized or transformed: NumDF, DenDF
## ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 5 remaining warnings.
#flower depth plot:
ggplot(all.years.morph, aes(x = factor(year), y = depth, fill = factor(year))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~plant, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Flower depth", fill = "Year")
## Warning: Removed 411 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

#average flower size 2024 vs 2025
#Q: Use anova or Anova???

avg.flower.size.all.years.model <- all.years.morph %>% 
  #galium has a lot of entries where depth, length & width all == NA
  filter(!if_all(c(depth, length, width), is.na)) %>%  
  #filter(!plant%in% "galium") %>% #galium doesn't have enough data
  group_by(plant) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model = map(data, ~ lmer(average_flower_size ~ factor(year) + (1|observer) + (1|transect), data = .x)),
    anova_table = map(model, ~ tidy(car::Anova(.x)))  # convert ANOVA to tibble
  ) %>%
  select(plant, anova_table) %>%
  unnest(anova_table) %>%
  mutate(sig = if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", ""))  # add * if p < 0.05

#flower depth plot:
ggplot(all.years.morph, aes(x = factor(year), y = average_flower_size, fill = factor(year))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~plant, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Avg Flower Size", fill = "Year")
## Warning: Removed 447 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

Visitation Rate

TODO: how to deal with different number of weeks observed? 2024 has like 6 weeks, 2025 has 12…. downsample? Total number of visits different? Average visits different? Visits to plant species different? Visits by poll groups different?

After standardizing for flower number, do we see differences in visits by insects?

#must run visitation-rate.Rmd and drought-net-2025.Rmd first to get vrate.complete and 

#change insect_class
#rename plant species
#add year column
#start 2024 over and filter it same as 2025????

library(hms)
## 
## Attaching package: 'hms'
## The following object is masked from 'package:lubridate':
## 
##     hms
vrate.cleaned.24 <- read_sheet("1N3HmtcG3Rv4smOS3-SWdpyHAuH3oLvK2KgzWG4JQmNY", na=c("na","","NA"))  %>% 
                mutate(week_num=factor(week_num)) %>% 
                select( -end, -notes, -noyce_data_entry) %>% 
                 mutate(insect_class= fct_collapse(insect_class, 
                       beetle = c("beetle", "beattle"), 
                       lepidoptera= c("butterfly", 
                                "checkerspot butterfly", "moth"), 
                       sol_bee= c( "bee", "carpenter bee"), 
                       fly=c("fly", "hover fly", "hoverfly",
                             "syrphid fly", "beefly", "bee fly"))) %>% 
                      #removed beetles and wasps from dataset
                      filter(!insect_class%in%c("beetle", "wasp")) %>%   
                      mutate(size= fct_collapse(size, 
                            big= c("l", "m", "m/l"), 
                            small = c("t","s", "t/s","s/t", "s/m"))) %>% 
                      #changes NAs in data, assigns NA for sol bee to small size class
                      mutate(size = if_else(is.na(size) & 
                                      insect_class== "sol_bee", "small", size)) %>% 
                      mutate(size = if_else(is.na(size) & 
                                      insect_class== "bombus", "big", size)) %>% 
                      mutate(size = if_else(is.na(size) & 
                                      insect_class== "lepidoptera", "big", size)) %>% 
                      #NOTE: many "fly" insect_class are NA but I don't know the size.... so put placeholder "unknown"
                      mutate(size = if_else(is.na(size) &
                                insect_class == "fly", "unkown", size)) %>% 
                      #assigns NAs (replaces the NaNs) to rows with no size data
                      mutate(insect_size=na_if(paste(size, insect_class), "NA NA")) %>% 
                      #Keep zeros bc you need then for observation.windows but add place holder for plant and insect_class:
                       #mutate(insect_class = if_else(
                        #        is.na(insect_class),
                        #        "no_insects",insect_class)) %>% #if insect_class is NA -> replace with no_insects
                      #mutate(plant = if_else(is.na(plant), "no_visits", plant)) %>% #if plant=NA replace with no_visits
                      #removing plants with little data
                      filter(!plant%in% c("vicia_americana","senecio_integerrimus",
                               "collomia_linearis","erigeron_speciosus",
                               "boechera_stricta","ent","micro white",
                               "campanula_rotundifolia", "eriogonum_subalpinum")) %>% 
                      #rename plant speieces to match flower counts
                      mutate(plant = dplyr::recode(plant,
                                            "eriogonum_umbellatum" = "umbellatum",
                                            "erigeron_flagellaris" = "flag",
                                            "potentilla_hippiana" = "potentilla",
                                            "heterotheca_villosa" = "heterotheca",
                                            "heliomeris_multiflora" = "heliomeris",
                                            "arenaria_congesta" = "congesta",
                                            "galium_boreale" = "galium")) %>% 
                      #remove columns to tidy data set
                      select(-insect_family, -pollinator_group) %>% 
                      #Creates new column flowers.per.plant.visited  
                      #divides # of visits by # of plants visited in that observation bout
                      mutate(flowers.per.plant.visited = case_when(
                            is.na(plants_visited) ~ NA_real_,
                            plants_visited == 0 ~ NA_real_,
                     #if number of plants visited is > number of visits, assign flowers.per.plant.visited ==1
                            plants_visited > visits ~ 1, 
                            TRUE ~ visits / plants_visited)) %>% 
                      rename(vrate24_week_num = week_num) %>% 
                      #fix start column to be time only
                      mutate(start = as_hms(start)) %>% 
                      #add calendar week column: 
                      mutate(calendar_week = week(date))  #adds calendar week based on the date column
## ✔ Reading from "Pollinator Observation Data".
## ✔ Range 'Sheet1'.
#prep for filling non-observed interactions with zeros in the data
#define all possible insect_classes
vrate24.insect.class <- vrate.cleaned.24 %>% 
  distinct(insect_class) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(insect_class))

# define all plants observed
vrate24.plants <- vrate.cleaned.24 %>%
    distinct(plant) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(plant))

# define observation windows
#one row per date × time × plot
#uses calendar_week instead of vrate24_week_num bc some week numbers are wrong
observation.windows.24 <- vrate.cleaned.24 %>%
    distinct(date,calendar_week, start,  
    transect, plot, transectplot)

vrate.24.complete <-  observation.windows.24 %>%
    #create all combinations of time x plants x insects (16,240 total possible combinations)
    #Note: this does not include insect size! 
    crossing(vrate24.plants, vrate24.insect.class) %>%
    left_join(
     vrate.cleaned.24,
      by = c(
        "date", "calendar_week", "start",
        "transect", "plot", "transectplot",
        "plant", "insect_class")) %>%
    mutate(visits = replace_na(visits, 0)) %>%  #makes all NA visits = 0
    mutate(plants_visited = replace_na(plants_visited, 0)) %>% 
    mutate(year = 2024) %>% 
    left_join(rainout.treatments, by = c("transect","transectplot", "plot")) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(treatment)) %>% 
    #Now must filter based on possible interactions where flower_number>0
    left_join(flowercounts.2024) %>% 
    #filter visits to only include an interaction if we recorded flower number for that plant during that week
    #create new column T/F if flowers of that plant are open on that date
    mutate(flower_present = !is.na(flower_number) & flower_number > 0) %>%
    #removes all rows with FALSE (no flowers open) (goes from 16,000 -> 3,500 rows...)  
    filter(flower_present) %>% 
    #remove umbellatum recorded in August because flowers aren't open
    filter(!(plant == "umbellatum" & date == as.Date("2024-08-08"))) %>% 
    # Visits per hour (for 15-min observation windows)
    mutate(visits.per.hr = visits * 4,
   # Visits per flower per hour: only if flower_number > 0
      visitsperflowerperhr = if_else(
      !is.na(flower_number) & flower_number > 0,
      (visits / flower_number) * 4,
      NA_real_)) 
## Joining with `by = join_by(date, transect, plot, transectplot, plant, year,
## treatment)`
  #remove insect_class = no_insect because isn't an observation
    #filter(!insect_class%in%"no_insects") #3,000 rows now
    #NOTE: is this the best way to do this? maybe shouldn't include option for no_insects above....



#..................... 2025 vrate ........................................
vrate.cleaned.25 <- read_sheet("1Fogbr6sgAxgfnCr5DWvaeCdJNHonhp_mn--mfSsvhCo") %>% 
                      mutate(week_num_vrate25=factor(week_num_vrate)) %>% 
                      select(-week_num_vrate, - end, -data_cleaning_notes, -notes) %>% 
                      #must include NA to keep observation windows with no interaction data
                      filter(plant%in% c(NA, focalspecies.2025)) %>% 
                      filter(!plant%in% "delphinium") %>% #no interaction data for delph
                      #rename plant species to match flower counts
                      mutate(plant = dplyr::recode(plant,
                                            "umb" = "umbellatum",
                                            "pot" = "potentilla",
                                            "het" = "heterotheca",
                                            "helio" = "heliomeris")) %>% 
                     #combine butterfly and moth observations
                      mutate(insect_class= fct_collapse(insect_class, 
                                  lepidoptera= c("butterfly", "moth"))) %>% 
                      filter(!insect_class%in% "wasp") %>%  #remove wasps
                      
                      mutate(start = as_hms(start)) %>% 
                      #adds calendar week based on the date column 
                      mutate(calendar_week = week(date))  %>% 
                      mutate(flowers.per.plant.visited = case_when(
                            is.na(plants_visited) ~ NA_real_,
                            plants_visited == 0 ~ NA_real_,
                     #if number of plants visited is > number of visits, assign flowers.per.plant.visited ==1
                            plants_visited > visits ~ 1, 
                            TRUE ~ visits / plants_visited)) 
## ✔ Reading from "visitation_rate_2025".
## ✔ Range 'origional data'.
#prep for filling non-observed interactions with zeros in the data
#define all possible insect_classes
vrate25.insect.class <- vrate.cleaned.25 %>% 
  distinct(insect_class) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(insect_class))

# define all plants observed
vrate25.plants <- vrate.cleaned.25 %>%
    distinct(plant) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(plant))

# define observation windows
#one row per date × time × plot
#uses calendar_week instead of vrate24_week_num bc some week numbers are wrong
observation.windows.25 <- vrate.cleaned.25 %>%
    distinct(date,calendar_week, start,  
    transect, plot, transectplot)

vrate.25.complete <-  observation.windows.25 %>%
    #create all combinations of time x plants x insects (16,240 total possible combinations)
    #Note: this does not include insect size! 
    crossing(vrate25.plants, vrate25.insect.class) %>%
    left_join(
     vrate.cleaned.25,
      by = c(
        "date", "calendar_week", "start",
        "transect", "plot", "transectplot",
        "plant", "insect_class")) %>%
    mutate(visits = replace_na(visits, 0)) %>%  #makes all NA visits = 0
    mutate(plants_visited = replace_na(plants_visited, 0)) %>% 
    mutate(year = 2025) %>% 
    left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
    #Now must filter based on possible interactions where flower_number>0
    left_join(flowercounts.2025 %>% select(-treatment, -year, -transect,-plot), by = c("date","transectplot","plant"),relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
    #filter visits to only include an interaction if we recorded flower number for that plant during that week
    #create new column T/F if flowers of that plant are open on that date
    mutate(flower_present = !is.na(flower_number) & flower_number > 0) %>%
    #removes all rows with FALSE (no flowers open) (goes from 16,000 -> 3,500 rows...)  
    filter(flower_present) %>% 
    #remove umbellatum recorded in August because flowers aren't open
    #filter(!(plant == "umbellatum" & date == as.Date("2024-08-08"))) %>% 
    # Visits per hour (for 15-min observation windows)
    mutate(visits.per.hr = visits * 4,
   # Visits per flower per hour: only if flower_number > 0
      visitsperflowerperhr = if_else(
      !is.na(flower_number) & flower_number > 0,
      (visits / flower_number) * 4,
      NA_real_))
## Joining with `by = join_by(transect, plot, transectplot)`
  #combine vrate 2024 and 2025
  #don't I need NAs for visits?? because you want every combination of insect and flower possible (based on open flowers). So there should be a lot of plant x insect with no visits..... both 2024 and 2025 data messed up need plant x insect combination with 0 visits

all.years.vrate <- bind_rows(vrate.24.complete, vrate.25.complete) %>% 
                    mutate(
                      year = factor(year),
                      plant = factor(plant),
                      transectplot = factor(transectplot)) %>% 
                    filter(!plant%in%"galium") #galium does not have enough visitation data for models


all.years.vrate %>% filter(!visits.per.hr== 0) %>% #remove zeros!
  ggplot(aes(x = year,
             y = visits.per.hr,
             color = plant)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(all.years.vrate,
       aes(x = treatment,
           y = visitsperflowerperhr,
           color = treatment)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = factor(year)), position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Treatment",
    y = "Visits per flower per hr",
    color = "Treatment",
    shape = "Year"
  )

##Models - vrate

#lmer
all.years.vrate.lmer <- all.years.vrate %>% 
          group_by(plant) %>%
          nest() %>%
          mutate(model = map(data, ~ lmer(visitsperflowerperhr ~ factor(year) + 
                          (1|calendar_week) +(1|transect/transectplot), data = .x)),
          anova_table = map(model, ~ tidy(anova(.x)))) %>%  # convert ANOVA to tibble
        select(plant, anova_table) %>%
        unnest(anova_table) %>%
        mutate(sig = if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", ""))  # add * if p < 0.05
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## Warning: There were 6 warnings in `mutate()`.
## The first warning was:
## ℹ In argument: `anova_table = map(model, ~tidy(anova(.x)))`.
## ℹ In group 1: `plant = congesta`.
## Caused by warning in `tidy.anova()`:
## ! The following column names in ANOVA output were not recognized or transformed: NumDF, DenDF
## ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 5 remaining warnings.
#glmmTMB with poisson distribution

#library(glmmTMB)

#glmmTMB with flower number in zi formula
fit_zipfull <- glmmTMB(visits ~ plant*year + flower_number + (1|transect), 
                ziformula = ~plant*year + flower_number + (1|transect),
                family = poisson, 
                data = all.years.vrate)
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
summary(fit_zipfull)
##  Family: poisson  ( log )
## Formula:          visits ~ plant * year + flower_number + (1 | transect)
## Zero inflation:          ~plant * year + flower_number + (1 | transect)
## Data: all.years.vrate
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##   14249.1   14440.2   -7096.5   14193.1      6786 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  transect (Intercept) 0.03602  0.1898  
## Number of obs: 6814, groups:  transect, 10
## 
## Zero-inflation model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  transect (Intercept) 0.04001  0.2     
## Number of obs: 6814, groups:  transect, 10
## 
## Conditional model:

## plantheliomeris            0.1487269  0.0775884   1.917 0.055255 .  
## plantheterotheca          -0.1672938  0.0656738  -2.547 0.010855 *  
## plantpotentilla           -0.3362325  0.0978253  -3.437 0.000588 ***
## plantumbellatum           -0.7358649  0.1184358  -6.213 5.19e-10 ***
## year2025                  -0.6951389  0.1484777  -4.682 2.84e-06 ***
## flower_number              0.0017079  0.0002757   6.195 5.82e-10 ***
## plantflag:year2025         0.0393136  0.2432103   0.162 0.871586    
## plantheliomeris:year2025  -0.2109640  0.1697663  -1.243 0.213988    
## plantheterotheca:year2025  0.4509004  0.1549206   2.911 0.003608 ** 
## plantpotentilla:year2025   0.5499878  0.1812685   3.034 0.002413 ** 
## plantumbellatum:year2025   0.1719863  0.2331901   0.738 0.460796

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Zero-inflation model:
##                             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)

## plantheliomeris           -0.4129321  0.1896898  -2.177   0.0295 *  
## plantheterotheca          -0.2911109  0.1579688  -1.843   0.0654 .  
## plantpotentilla           -0.3554130  0.1942015  -1.830   0.0672 .  
## plantumbellatum           -0.1206303  0.2015823  -0.598   0.5496    
## year2025                   1.6805589  0.2591358   6.485 8.86e-11 ***
## flower_number             -0.0125862  0.0007146 -17.614  < 2e-16 ***
## plantflag:year2025        -1.9034367  0.3712703  -5.127 2.95e-07 ***
## plantheliomeris:year2025  -1.8626924  0.3156531  -5.901 3.61e-09 ***
## plantheterotheca:year2025 -1.6391268  0.2810396  -5.832 5.46e-09 ***
## plantpotentilla:year2025  -1.4496245  0.3169160  -4.574 4.78e-06 ***
## plantumbellatum:year2025  -1.4065864  0.3482768  -4.039 5.37e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#glmmTMB without flower number in zi formula
#In fit_zip, only plant and year influence the probability of getting zero visits.

fit_zip <- glmmTMB(visits ~ plant*year + flower_number + (1|transect),
              ziformula = ~plant*year, 
              family = poisson, 
              data = all.years.vrate)
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
summary(fit_zip)
##  Family: poisson  ( log )
## Formula:          visits ~ plant * year + flower_number + (1 | transect)
## Zero inflation:          ~plant * year
## Data: all.years.vrate
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid

## 
## Random effects:

##  transect (Intercept) 0.03826  0.1956

## plantumbellatum            0.007262   0.196937   0.037 0.970583    
## year2025                   1.172911   0.244213   4.803 1.56e-06 ***
## plantflag:year2025        -1.225182   0.340724  -3.596 0.000323 ***
## plantheliomeris:year2025  -1.028241   0.296788  -3.465 0.000531 ***
## plantheterotheca:year2025 -0.928872   0.262335  -3.541 0.000399 ***
## plantpotentilla:year2025  -0.918393   0.303924  -3.022 0.002513 ** 
## plantumbellatum:year2025  -0.906924   0.337861  -2.684 0.007268 **

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#find model with lowest AIC:
AIC(fit_zipfull, fit_zip)
##             df      AIC
## fit_zipfull 28 14249.07
## fit_zip     26 14658.11
fit_zipdropinteraction <- glmmTMB(visits ~ year + plant + flower_number + (1|transect), 
                            ziformula = ~ year + plant, 
                            family = poisson, 
                            data = all.years.vrate)
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
anova(fit_zipfull, fit_zipdropinteraction) #will do a likelihood ratio test of the effect of the interaction.
## Data: all.years.vrate
## Models:
## fit_zipdropinteraction: visits ~ year + plant + flower_number + (1 | transect), zi=~year + plant, disp=~1
## fit_zipfull: visits ~ plant * year + flower_number + (1 | transect), zi=~plant * year + flower_number + (1 | transect), disp=~1
##                        Df   AIC   BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)
## fit_zipdropinteraction 16 14723 14832 -7345.6    14691                         
## fit_zipfull            28 14249 14440 -7096.5    14193 498.17     12  < 2.2e-16
##                           
## fit_zipdropinteraction    
## fit_zipfull            ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

##2025 treatment model

#2025 visitation rate treatment glmmTMB model:

#glmmTMB with flower number in zi formula
fit_zipfull_treatment_25 <- glmmTMB(visits ~ plant*treatment + flower_number + (1|transect), 
                ziformula = ~plant*treatment + flower_number + (1|transect),
                family = poisson, 
                data = all.years.vrate %>% filter(year==2025))
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
summary(fit_zipfull_treatment_25)
##  Family: poisson  ( log )
## Formula:          visits ~ plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect)
## Zero inflation:          ~plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect)
## Data: all.years.vrate %>% filter(year == 2025)
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    7067.0    7243.1   -3505.5    7011.0      3953 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  transect (Intercept) 0.06281  0.2506  
## Number of obs: 3981, groups:  transect, 10
## 
## Zero-inflation model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  transect (Intercept) 0.01587  0.126   
## Number of obs: 3981, groups:  transect, 10
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                        1.0408779  0.1987165   5.238 1.62e-07 ***
## plantflag                         -1.6448243  0.3510289  -4.686 2.79e-06 ***
## plantheliomeris                   -0.2724932  0.1869578  -1.458 0.144975    
## plantheterotheca                  -0.0367929  0.1674154  -0.220 0.826050    
## plantpotentilla                   -0.1598779  0.2018603  -0.792 0.428348    
## plantumbellatum                   -0.9306027  0.2573707  -3.616 0.000299 ***
## treatmentrainout                  -1.0516567  0.3687423  -2.852 0.004344 ** 
## flower_number                      0.0016872  0.0003964   4.256 2.08e-05 ***
## plantflag:treatmentrainout         1.8505545  0.4925740   3.757 0.000172 ***
## plantheliomeris:treatmentrainout   0.6581094  0.3802782   1.731 0.083523 .  
## plantheterotheca:treatmentrainout  0.9347705  0.3359895   2.782 0.005400 ** 
## plantpotentilla:treatmentrainout   1.0904351  0.3762397   2.898 0.003753 ** 
## plantumbellatum:treatmentrainout   1.2935869  0.4512726   2.867 0.004150 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Zero-inflation model:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                        3.4832829  0.2993080  11.638  < 2e-16 ***
## plantflag                         -2.2438455  0.4883270  -4.595 4.33e-06 ***
## plantheliomeris                   -2.0276369  0.3344906  -6.062 1.35e-09 ***
## plantheterotheca                  -1.9717821  0.3031415  -6.504 7.80e-11 ***
## plantpotentilla                   -1.6438557  0.3443643  -4.774 1.81e-06 ***
## plantumbellatum                   -1.3512472  0.3787548  -3.568  0.00036 ***
## treatmentrainout                  -0.3862698  0.4680452  -0.825  0.40921    
## flower_number                     -0.0099609  0.0009128 -10.913  < 2e-16 ***
## plantflag:treatmentrainout         1.1461782  0.6444162   1.779  0.07530 .  
## plantheliomeris:treatmentrainout  -0.1999611  0.5345733  -0.374  0.70836    
## plantheterotheca:treatmentrainout  0.3338875  0.4793361   0.697  0.48608    
## plantpotentilla:treatmentrainout   0.2154479  0.5261718   0.409  0.68220    
## plantumbellatum:treatmentrainout   0.2506256  0.5778808   0.434  0.66451    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#glmmTMB without flower number in zi formula
#In fit_zip, only plant and year influence the probability of getting zero visits.

fit_zip_treatment_25 <- glmmTMB(visits ~ plant*treatment + flower_number + (1|transect),
              ziformula = ~plant*treatment, 
              family = poisson, 
              data = all.years.vrate %>% filter(year==2025))
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
#find model with lowest AIC:
AIC(fit_zipfull_treatment_25, fit_zip_treatment_25)
##                          df      AIC
## fit_zipfull_treatment_25 28 7066.981
## fit_zip_treatment_25     26 7215.411
fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_25 <- glmmTMB(visits ~ treatment + plant + flower_number + (1|transect), 
                            ziformula = ~ treatment + plant, 
                            family = poisson, 
                            data = all.years.vrate %>% filter(year==2025))
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
anova(fit_zipfull_treatment_25, fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_25)
## Data: all.years.vrate %>% filter(year == 2025)
## Models:
## fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_25: visits ~ treatment + plant + flower_number + (1 | transect), zi=~treatment + plant, disp=~1
## fit_zipfull_treatment_25: visits ~ plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect), zi=~plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect), disp=~1
##                                     Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq
## fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_25 16 7220.1 7320.7 -3594.0   7188.1       
## fit_zipfull_treatment_25            28 7067.0 7243.1 -3505.5   7011.0 177.11
##                                     Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_25                      
## fit_zipfull_treatment_25                12  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

##2024 treatment model

#2024 visitation rate treatment glmmTMB model:

#glmmTMB with flower number in zi formula
fit_zipfull_treatment_24 <- glmmTMB(visits ~ plant*treatment + flower_number + (1|transect), 
                ziformula = ~plant*treatment + flower_number + (1|transect),
                family = poisson, 
                data = all.years.vrate %>% filter(year==2024))
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
summary(fit_zipfull_treatment_24)
##  Family: poisson  ( log )
## Formula:          visits ~ plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect)
## Zero inflation:          ~plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect)
## Data: all.years.vrate %>% filter(year == 2024)
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    7138.9    7305.5   -3541.5    7082.9      2805 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  transect (Intercept) 0.0302   0.1738  
## Number of obs: 2833, groups:  transect, 10
## 
## Zero-inflation model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  transect (Intercept) 0.03097  0.176   
## Number of obs: 2833, groups:  transect, 10
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                    Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                        1.303186   0.131914   9.879  < 2e-16 ***
## plantflag                         -0.707119   0.235678  -3.000 0.002697 ** 
## plantheliomeris                    0.345599   0.125692   2.750 0.005967 ** 
## plantheterotheca                  -0.102843   0.115879  -0.888 0.374807    
## plantpotentilla                    0.008325   0.156600   0.053 0.957606    
## plantumbellatum                   -0.356361   0.158547  -2.248 0.024597 *  
## treatmentrainout                   0.224682   0.171474   1.310 0.190095    
## flower_number                      0.002155   0.000417   5.168 2.36e-07 ***
## plantflag:treatmentrainout        -0.676776   0.299778  -2.258 0.023971 *  
## plantheliomeris:treatmentrainout  -0.697076   0.179014  -3.894 9.86e-05 ***
## plantheterotheca:treatmentrainout -0.220075   0.139771  -1.575 0.115363    
## plantpotentilla:treatmentrainout  -0.827405   0.214969  -3.849 0.000119 ***
## plantumbellatum:treatmentrainout  -1.181809   0.276699  -4.271 1.95e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Zero-inflation model:
##                                    Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                        2.440296   0.224310  10.879   <2e-16 ***
## plantflag                         -0.312882   0.350142  -0.894   0.3715    
## plantheliomeris                   -0.519237   0.305253  -1.701   0.0889 .  
## plantheterotheca                  -0.373634   0.250852  -1.489   0.1364    
## plantpotentilla                   -0.194722   0.340248  -0.572   0.5671    
## plantumbellatum                   -0.449408   0.290188  -1.549   0.1215    
## treatmentrainout                  -0.730373   0.306966  -2.379   0.0173 *  
## flower_number                     -0.015721   0.001104 -14.239   <2e-16 ***
## plantflag:treatmentrainout         0.423590   0.461398   0.918   0.3586    
## plantheliomeris:treatmentrainout   0.237225   0.412247   0.575   0.5650    
## plantheterotheca:treatmentrainout  0.468114   0.326124   1.435   0.1512    
## plantpotentilla:treatmentrainout  -0.349037   0.434168  -0.804   0.4214    
## plantumbellatum:treatmentrainout   0.198903   0.440776   0.451   0.6518    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#glmmTMB without flower number in zi formula
#In fit_zip, only plant and year influence the probability of getting zero visits.

fit_zip_treatment_24 <- glmmTMB(visits ~ plant*treatment + flower_number + (1|transect),
              ziformula = ~plant*treatment, 
              family = poisson, 
              data = all.years.vrate %>% filter(year==2024))
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
#find model with lowest AIC:
AIC(fit_zipfull_treatment_24, fit_zip_treatment_24)
##                          df      AIC
## fit_zipfull_treatment_24 28 7138.910
## fit_zip_treatment_24     26 7385.442
fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_24 <- glmmTMB(visits ~ treatment + plant + flower_number + (1|transect), 
                            ziformula = ~ treatment + plant, 
                            family = poisson, 
                            data = all.years.vrate %>% filter(year==2024))
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
anova(fit_zipfull_treatment_24, fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_24)
## Data: all.years.vrate %>% filter(year == 2024)
## Models:
## fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_24: visits ~ treatment + plant + flower_number + (1 | transect), zi=~treatment + plant, disp=~1
## fit_zipfull_treatment_24: visits ~ plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect), zi=~plant * treatment + flower_number + (1 | transect), disp=~1
##                                     Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq
## fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_24 16 7424.1 7519.3 -3696.0   7392.1       
## fit_zipfull_treatment_24            28 7138.9 7305.5 -3541.5   7082.9 309.16
##                                     Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## fit_zipdropinteraction_treatment_24                      
## fit_zipfull_treatment_24                12  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#TODO: the anova p-value is the same for all of the models...

#difference in observed insect visits (relative amount of visits in each year)

#2024 relative amounts of observed insect visits
relative.insects.24 <- vrate.24.complete %>%   count(insect_class) %>%
  mutate(relative_proportion = n / sum(n))

#2025
relative.insects.25 <- vrate.25.complete %>%   count(insect_class) %>%
  mutate(relative_proportion = n / sum(n))

#NOTE: proportion of visits per insect_class is not different between years.

#relative insect visits per plant species

#Relative proportion of all insect visits to plant species (NOT STADARDIZED by flower number)
#proportion of visits by an insect per year out of that year's total
relative.global.by.year <- all.years.vrate %>% 
  group_by(year, plant, insect_class) %>% 
  summarise(total_visits = sum(visits), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  group_by(year) %>% 
  mutate(relative_proportion = round(total_visits / sum(total_visits), 3)) %>% 
  ungroup()

ggplot(relative.global.by.year,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_proportion,
           fill = insect_class)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative proportion of all visits",
    fill = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

#Standardize relative proportion of visits to plants by flower number
#Standardize within plant first, then global to remove plant abundance effects but still compare insects
relative.by.plant.flower <- all.years.vrate %>% 
  group_by(year, plant, insect_class) %>% 
  summarise(
    total_visits = sum(visits),
    flowers = mean(flower_number),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(visits_per_flower = total_visits / flowers) %>% 
  group_by(year, plant) %>% 
  mutate(relative_within_plant = visits_per_flower / sum(visits_per_flower)) %>% 
  ungroup()

#DON'T USE! only for visualization purposes - use relative.global.plant.flower.by.year below
ggplot(relative.by.plant.flower,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_within_plant,
           fill = insect_class)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative visits per flower",
    fill = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

#USE THIS: 
#For a single year, what fraction of all flower-standardized visits went to each plant × insect combination, relative to the total for that year?
#Insect visits to plants standardized by flower number
#global proportion of insect visits to each plant species within a single year that is standardized by flower number
##Sum the visits_per_flower across all plants for each insect class within a year.
##Divide by the total visits_per_flower for that year to get the proportion.

relative.global.plant.flower.by.year <- relative.by.plant.flower %>%
                      group_by(year, plant, insect_class) %>% #all standardized visits per plant × insect are summed
                      summarise(total_visits_per_flower = sum(visits_per_flower),
                                  .groups = "drop") %>%
                      group_by(year) %>% #the denominator is all visits per flower in that year, across all plants and insects
                      mutate(relative_proportion = round(total_visits_per_flower / sum(total_visits_per_flower), 3)) %>%
                      ungroup()


ggplot(relative.global.plant.flower.by.year,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_proportion,
           fill = insect_class)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative visits per flower",
    fill = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggplot(relative.global.plant.flower.by.year,
       aes(x = factor(year),
           y = relative_proportion,
           color = insect_class,
           group = insect_class)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ plant) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Relative visits per flower",
    color = "Insect class"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

#network ##2024

net.2024 <- vrate.24.complete %>% 
            group_by(plant, insect_class) %>% 
            summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% 
            drop_na(plant) %>% 
            pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
            arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
            column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
            relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars()))  #orders columns alphabetically
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'plant'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
#bipartite network plot:                          
pp.network.24 <- plotweb(sqrt(net.2024), empty=F, text.rot=90, method="normal")

#network statistics
net.24.stats <- networklevel(net.2024)

##2025

net.2025 <- vrate.25.complete %>% 
            group_by(plant, insect_class) %>% 
            summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% 
            drop_na(plant) %>% 
            pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
            arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
            column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
            relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars()))  #orders columns alphabetically
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'plant'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
#bipartite network plot:                          
pp.network.25 <- plotweb(sqrt(net.2025), empty=F, text.rot=90, method="normal")

#network statistics
net.25.stats <- networklevel(net.2025)

#downsample networks

###2024

#down sampling allows you to have same number of interactions in both control and drought so you can compare the two networks more easily
#take net.2024 and net.2025 and take random rows

#number to down sample to
#figure out number to downsample to:
vrate.24.complete %>% count(treatment)
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   treatment     n
##   <chr>     <int>
## 1 control    1493
## 2 rainout    1612
vrate.25.complete %>% count(treatment)
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   treatment     n
##   <chr>     <int>
## 1 control    2250
## 2 rainout    1967
downsample.rows <- 1000 #number of rows in down sampled networks
#down sample number based on number of interactions in 2024 control 

#make mini networks 

downsample.function.2024 <- function(manipulation, downsample.rows){
                          vrate.24.complete %>%  
                          #left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
                          filter(treatment== manipulation) %>% 
                          slice_sample(n=downsample.rows) %>% 
                          group_by(plant, insect_class) %>% summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% drop_na(plant) %>% 
                          pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
                          arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
                          column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
                          relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars())) %>%  #orders columns alphabetically
                          networklevel(index=c("connectance", "NODF", "H2", "links per species")) %>% 
                          t() %>% data.frame()
                        }

stats.24.downsample <- tibble(permutation=rep(1:99, times=2), treatment=rep(c("control", "rainout"), each=99)) %>% 
              mutate(stats= map(treatment, ~downsample.function.2024(.x, downsample.rows))) %>% unnest(stats)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'plant'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'plant'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'plant'. You can override using the

## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'plant'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
# Links per species plot (species = insect class and size)
dnet.color.palette <- c(control="cornflowerblue", rainout="orange")
year.color.palette <- c("2024" = "darkgreen", "2025"= "purple")


ggplot(stats.24.downsample, aes(x=links.per.species, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)

ggplot(stats.24.downsample, aes(x=connectance, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)

#t test
#don't use pvalue bc we are just choosing how much data to create with number of permutations
anova(lm(links.per.species~treatment, data=stats.24.downsample))
## Analysis of Variance Table
## 
## Response: links.per.species
##            Df  Sum Sq  Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
## treatment   1 0.12307 0.123069  13.315 0.0003375 ***
## Residuals 196 1.81156 0.009243                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

###2025

downsample.function.2025 <- function(manipulation, downsample.rows){
                          vrate.25.complete %>%  
                          #left_join(rainout.treatments) %>% 
                          filter(treatment== manipulation) %>% 
                          #randomly selects a subset of rows
                          slice_sample(n=downsample.rows) %>% 
                          #aggregate visits 
                          group_by(plant, insect_class) %>%
                          summarise(visits=sum(visits)) %>% 
                          #removes missing plant names
                          drop_na(plant) %>% 
                          #convert to an interaction matrix
                          pivot_wider(names_from=insect_class, values_from = visits, values_fill = 0) %>%  
                          arrange(plant) %>% #orders rows (plant names) alphabetically
                          column_to_rownames("plant") %>% 
                          relocate(sort(tidyselect::peek_vars())) %>%  #orders columns alphabetically
                          networklevel(index=c("connectance", "NODF", "H2", "links per species")) %>% 
                          t() %>% data.frame()
                        }

#999 permutations for each treatment
stats.25.downsample <- tibble(permutation=rep(1:99, times=2), treatment=rep(c("control", "rainout"), each=99)) %>% 
                        mutate(stats= map(treatment, ~downsample.function.2025(.x, downsample.rows))) %>% 
                        unnest(stats) #Expands the list column so each permutation becomes a normal row with network metrics

# Links per species plot (species = insect class and size - TODO: does it? might need to double check)
ggplot(stats.25.downsample, aes(x=links.per.species, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)

ggplot(stats.25.downsample, aes(x=connectance, fill= treatment))+ geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) + scale_fill_manual(values=dnet.color.palette) + facet_wrap(vars(treatment), ncol = 1)
